behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: PR-change discovery -> file-selection -> analyzer dispatch. An in-process test with fixture diff metadata and a spy analyzer can catch real regressions (e.g., unchanged files leaking into scan set). Rejected System: behavior text only says "changed in the pull request" vs "unchanged files are not scanned" and does not require validating real network/OS/subprocess effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real change-filtering + scan-orchestration modules together. Feed a mixed fixture (changed and unchanged paths) and assert only changed paths are passed into analysis; unchanged files must never be invoked.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic plumbing across modules: finding aggregation -> severity bucketing -> comment renderer/publisher payload. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on grouping in "posted PR comment," which can be validated via mocked GitHub client payload without real HTTP. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI decision content is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run aggregator + comment-formatter + publish adapter with deterministic findings spanning error/warning/info. Assert outbound comment body/payload contains correct three grouped sections and memberships.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is CI runtime under real process/IO conditions and wall-clock budget. Mocked timing or fake executors can hide contention, startup overhead, and filesystem/process costs. This explicitly references GitHub Actions timeout, which is an environment-level constraint.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step end-to-end in CI-like conditions against a generated 200-file change set, invoking the real linter process and timing total wall-clock duration. Fail if runtime exceeds 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real subprocess crash semantics (exit/signals/failure isolation) plus continuation logic. In-process exception fakes can miss real crash handling differences. Rejected Integration: text explicitly says "linter process crashes," which is subprocess/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration against a corpus including one malformed file that triggers an actual linter crash. Verify pipeline records the failure, continues analyzing remaining files, and still emits final PR results/comment payload.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Likely failure appears at parser/normalizer/deduper/output boundary, not just one pure function, because duplicates come from overlapping rule outputs and must be removed in final emitted results. Rejected System: behavior is about output dedup semantics and does not require real network/OS/subprocess to reproduce. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real parsing + aggregation + dedup + output formatting modules with fixture findings that intentionally collide on identical file/line/rule targets. Assert final emitted output contains one canonical entry per duplicate set.
